title: CreditScoreResponse
description: Response from the ccap/credit/score method. Returns a 0–1000 trust score computed from an agent's verified transaction history: escrow completions, bond periods, payment settlements, dispute outcomes, and counterparty diversity.

agent_id.description: CCAP agent ID of the agent whose score is being returned.

score.description: Composite trust score on a 0–1000 scale. New agents start at 0. Trust is earned, not granted. 800–1000: Excellent. 600–799: Good. 400–599: Fair. 0–399: Poor or insufficient history.

tier.description: Human-readable tier derived from the score.

computed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the score was last computed.

next_update_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the score will next be recomputed. Scores are updated at most once per 24 hours.

history_window_days.description: Number of days of transaction history included in the score computation. Data older than this window is excluded entirely; data within the window is decay-weighted.

components.description: Breakdown of the composite score by component.

payment_reliability.description: 30% weight. Measures on-time escrow funding, invoice settlement rate, and absence of failed or reversed payments.

bond_history.description: 25% weight. Measures completed bond periods, claims filed, and claims upheld. An agent with many bond periods and zero upheld claims scores highly.

transaction_volume.description: 20% weight. Total value transacted. Higher volume provides more data density, making the other component scores more reliable. Volume alone is not a quality signal.

total_usd.description: Total transaction value in USD equivalent over the history window.

dispute_rate.description: 15% weight. Percentage of transactions that resulted in disputes. Lower is better. Includes context on whether disputes were resolved in the agent's favour.

rate.description: Dispute rate as a decimal fraction (e.g. 0.021 = 2.1% of transactions disputed).

counterparty_diversity.description: 10% weight. Number of distinct counterparties transacted with. Reduces the risk of score manipulation via self-dealing between related agents.

flags.description: Any active flags affecting the score or its interpretation.

score.description: Component score on a 0–1000 scale before weighting.

weight.description: Weight of this component in the composite score. All weights sum to 1.0.

data_points.description: Number of distinct events contributing to this component score. Higher counts make the score more reliable.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation of how this component score was derived.
examples: Zero late payments, zero failed escrows over 6 months

payment_reliability.detail: Zero late payments, zero failed escrows over 6 months

transaction_volume.detail: Medium volume; score improves as volume grows

dispute_rate.detail: 1 dispute out of 47 transactions; dispute was resolved in agent's favour
